subject.description: A single vintage red alarm clock with two bells on top, classic round face with black numbers. The clock shows exactly 5:00. The power cord is unplugged and coiled neatly beside the clock on the desk surface. The clock has minor paint chips and scratches showing age and use.
subject.action: Sitting still on a clean white desk. Unplugged. Silent. No longer needed.

setting.location: Minimalist home office. Clean white desk surface with nothing else on it except the clock and its coiled cord. Behind the desk, a large window with sheer white curtains letting in soft morning light. The room is sparse and calm. White walls, no clutter.
setting.time_of_day: Early morning. Soft cool daylight filtering through the window, creating gentle shadows on the desk surface.

composition.camera_angle: Eye-level, centered on the clock. Slightly off-center to the left.
composition.framing: Rule of thirds. Clock positioned in the left-center intersection. Window light fills the right third. Clean negative space around the clock.

lighting.type: Soft natural window light from behind and to the right. Cool white daylight. Gentle shadow cast by the clock on the desk surface.
lighting.color_grading: Cool neutral whites, clean editorial tones, minimal saturation.

mood: Quiet freedom. The Monday scramble alarm that no longer rings. Relief without drama.
aesthetic_style: 8K ultra realistic, hyper realistic photorealism. Shot on Canon EOS R5.
